name: Bosque de Tejedelo — circular by Requejo de Sanabria and natural park Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores
name_original: Bosque de Tejedelo — circular por Requejo de Sanabria y parque natural Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores
region_intro: Hiking around Requejo offers access to a diverse mountainous landscape within the province of Zamora, Spain. The region is characterized by the Sierra Segundera to the north and the Sierra de la Parada to the south, with the Requejo river carving a central valley. Elevations range significantly, providing varied terrain for hikers. A notable feature is the ancient Bosque del Tejedelo, an important yew grove, and the area is situated within the broader Sanabria Lake Natural Park, known for its glacial lakes and rich biodiversity. Best hiking trails around Requejo The most popular hiking route is PR3 Porto Furado Trail, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate path showcases granite formations and reservoir views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Bosque de Tejedelo — circular by Requejo de Sanabria and natural park Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores, an easy 3.0 miles (4.7 km) path. This route leads through an ancient yew grove, a significant natural feature of the region. Local hikers also love the Waterfall and Lagoon of Sotillo from Sotillo de Sanabria — natural park Lake of Sanabria and sierras Segundera and Porto, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail leading through the Sanabria Lake Natural Park, often completed in about 5 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Requejo is defined by mountainous terrain, ancient yew forests, and glacial lakes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Requejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 960 hikers have used komoot to explore Requejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Requejo?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails around Requejo, offering a diverse range of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging mountain treks. The region's mountainous terrain, ancient yew forests, and glacial lakes provide varied landscapes for exploration.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners in Requejo?
A: Yes, Requejo offers several easy hiking trails perfect for families or those new to hiking. One excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022985701/bosque-de-tejedelo-circular-by-requejo-de-sanabria-and-natural-park-lago-de-sanabria-y-alrededores" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de Tejedelo — circular by Requejo de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.7 km path that leads through an ancient and well-preserved yew grove, offering a unique natural experience.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Requejo?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in the Requejo area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022985701/bosque-de-tejedelo-circular-by-requejo-de-sanabria-and-natural-park-lago-de-sanabria-y-alrededores" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de Tejedelo — circular by Requejo de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy circular route, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1328058759/cascadas-de-sotillo-circular-desde-sotillo-de-sanabria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascadas de Sotillo — circular desde Sotillo de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate circular option featuring waterfalls.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in the Requejo area?
A: Yes, the region is home to beautiful waterfalls. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022032424/waterfall-and-lagoon-of-sotillo-from-sotillo-de-sanabria-natural-park-lake-of-sanabria-and-sierras-segundera-and-porto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Lagoon of Sotillo from Sotillo de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging route leading to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023013364/cascadas-de-aguas-cernidas-round-trip-from-terroso-through-the-natural-park-lago-de-sanabria-and-sierras-segundera-and-de-porto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascadas de Aguas Cernidas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate round trip from Terroso.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Requejo?
A: Requejo's landscape is predominantly mountainous, characterized by the Sierra Segundera and Sierra de la Parada. You'll encounter ancient forests, most notably the Bosque del Tejedelo with its thousand-year-old yew trees, and the area is part of the Sanabria Lake Natural Park, featuring the largest glacial lake on the Iberian Peninsula and numerous smaller glacial lakes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can visit several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent natural attraction. For historical interest, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/910086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Counts of Benavente&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town of Puebla de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt; are worth exploring. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of La Tuiza&lt;/a&gt; also offers a cultural stop.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Requejo?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Requejo, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse mountainous terrain, the mystical atmosphere of the ancient yew groves, and the stunning glacial landscapes of the Sanabria Lake Natural Park.

Q: Is Requejo part of any long-distance walking routes?
A: Yes, Requejo is located on the Camino Sanabrés, also known as the Camino Mozárabe. This is a significant pilgrimage route to Santiago de Compostela, meaning you'll find well-established paths and amenities catering to walkers, including hostels and places for dining.

Q: Are there good viewpoints along the hiking trails in Requejo?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain offers numerous opportunities for panoramic views. The Bosque del Tejedelo features a viewpoint called "Peña del Valadero" offering expansive views of the yew and oak forest. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Lake Sanabria&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning vistas over the largest glacial lake in the Iberian Peninsula.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Requejo?
A: The best season for hiking in Requejo generally depends on your preference. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, making them ideal for exploring the forests and mountains. Summer is also popular, especially for enjoying the glacial lakes, though some higher altitude trails might be warmer. Winter can be beautiful with snow, but requires appropriate gear and caution on certain routes.

Q: Are there any trails that are less crowded for a more peaceful hiking experience?
A: While popular spots like the Bosque del Tejedelo can see visitors, the extensive network of trails in the Sierra Segundera and Sierra de la Parada, particularly those leading to smaller glacial lakes or higher peaks, often provide a more secluded experience. Exploring routes further into the Sanabria Lake Natural Park beyond the main attractions can also lead to quieter paths.
tips: In the Arroyo Tejedelo Recreational Area we will find two wooden tables and benches to recharge energy and continue with our route. It is located in an unbeatable place, in a beautiful setting near a wooden bridge over the Tejedelo stream.

Following the route along an ascending path, we will arrive at this beautiful viewpoint, from which we can see the wonderful panorama of Tejedelo, a mixed forest dominated by oaks and holly where the dark crowns of the yew trees stand out.
Following the path marked with the beacons, we enter the forest of "El Tejedelo" where we will be accompanied by majestic ancient yew trees, which appear like giants on the sides of the path.
